articles.title: Trump criticises Nato as alliance chief describes meeting as 'very frank'
articles.description: The US president says Nato "wasn't there when we needed them" during the Iran war.
articles.titleJa: トランプ氏がNATOを批判、同盟首長は会談を「非常に率直」と表現
articles.summary: 米大統領がイラン戦争時にNATOが支援しなかったと批判し、同盟首長は会談を「非常に率直だった」と述べた。

articles.title: Iran ceasefire deal gives Trump a way out of war - but at a high cost
articles.description: The path to the two-week ceasefire with Iran may have fundamentally altered the way the rest of the world views the US.
articles.titleJa: イラン停戦合意はトランプ氏に戦争からの出口を与えるが、代償は大きい
articles.summary: イランとの2週間の停戦合意は、トランプ米大統領に戦争からの出口を与えたが、その代償は大きく、世界の対米観を根本的に変えた可能性がある。

articles.title: What we know about the two-week ceasefire between the US and Iran
articles.description: The provisional truce comes more than a month after the US and Israel launched coordinated attacks on Iran.
articles.titleJa: 米国とイラン間の2週間の停戦についてわかっていること
articles.summary: 米国とイスラエルがイランへの協調攻撃を開始してから1ヶ月以上を経て、暫定的な停戦が成立した。

articles.title: At least 182 killed across Lebanon in large wave of Israeli strikes
articles.description: Attacks hit the southern suburbs of Beirut, southern Lebanon and the eastern Bekaa Valley, hours after a US-Iran ceasefire was announced.
articles.titleJa: 米イラン停戦発表数時間後、レバノン全土でイスラエル軍の空爆により少なくとも182人死亡
articles.summary: 米イラン停戦発表から数時間後、ベイルート南部郊外、レバノン南部、ベッカー渓谷東部がイスラエル軍の攻撃を受け、少なくとも182人が死亡した。

articles.title: He's Australia's most decorated soldier. Now he's at the centre of a historic war crimes case
articles.description: Ben Roberts-Smith's case is not only unprecedented for Australia but "extraordinary" for the globe too, scholars say.
articles.titleJa: 豪州で最も勲章を受けた兵士、歴史的な戦争犯罪事件の中心に
articles.summary: 豪州で最も勲章を受けた兵士が、豪州だけでなく世界的にも「異例」とされる歴史的な戦争犯罪事件の中心にいる。

articles.title: Prosecutors seek Tiger Woods' prescription drug records after Florida arrest
articles.description: A legal order seeking the golfer's medications - including dosage and warnings about driving on pill bottles - will be issued later this month, court record shows.
articles.titleJa: フロリダでの逮捕後、検察官がタイガー・ウッズ選手の処方薬記録を請求
articles.summary: フロリダでの逮捕後、検察官がゴルフ選手タイガー・ウッズの処方薬記録（服用量や運転に関する警告を含む）の開示命令を今月後半に発行する予定だ。

articles.title: Greece to ban social media for under-15s from next year
articles.description: It follows similar moves in other European countries, including France and Spain.
articles.titleJa: ギリシャ、来年から15歳未満のソーシャルメディア利用を禁止へ
articles.summary: ギリシャは、フランスやスペインなど他のヨーロッパ諸国と同様に、来年から15歳未満のソーシャルメディア利用を禁止する予定だ。

articles.title: Germany suspends military approval for long stays abroad for men under 45
articles.description: The requirement to obtain permission had gone largely unnoticed until a German newspaper reported on it.
articles.titleJa: ドイツ、45歳未満男性の長期海外滞在軍事承認を停止
articles.summary: ドイツが、45歳未満の男性に対し、海外での長期滞在に必要な軍事承認の要件を停止した。

articles.title: Pioneering wildlife cameraman Doug Allan dies in Nepal
articles.description: The cameraman and photographer won eight Emmy Awards for his work on acclaimed series like Blue Planet alongside Sir David Attenborough.
articles.titleJa: 先駆的な野生動物カメラマン、ダグ・アラン氏がネパールで死去
articles.summary: 「ブルー・プラネット」などのシリーズでエミー賞を8回受賞した先駆的な野生動物カメラマン、ダグ・アラン氏がネパールで死去した。

articles.title: Iran Strait of Hormuz warning adds to shipping uncertainty
articles.description: Only a few vessels have crossed the strait since the US-Iran ceasefire deal, according to BBC Verify analysis.
articles.titleJa: イランのホルムズ海峡警告が海運の不確実性を増大
articles.summary: イランがホルムズ海峡に関して警告を発し、米イラン停戦合意以来、海峡を通過した船舶が少ないことから、海運の不確実性が増している。

articles.title: Iran threatens new hostilities as Israel pounds Lebanon
articles.description: The two-week truce between the US, Israel and Iran has started showing cracks as the sides disagreed on Lebanon's inclusion in the ceasefire agreement. Follow DW for more.
articles.titleJa: イラン、イスラエルがレバノンを攻撃する中、新たな敵対行為を警告
articles.summary: 米・イスラエル・イラン間の停戦に亀裂が生じ、イスラエルがレバノンを空爆。イランは新たな敵対行為を警告し、停戦におけるレバノンの扱いが焦点に。

articles.title: Trump criticizes NATO over Iran in meeting with Rutte
articles.description: President Donald Trump said NATO "wasn't there" after the US and Israel launched attacks on Iran. But NATO head Mark Rutte said "a large majority of European countries" lived up to their side of the deal.
articles.titleJa: トランプ氏、ルッテ氏との会談でイラン巡りNATOを批判
articles.summary: トランプ大統領は、米国とイスラエルのイラン攻撃後、NATOが「そこにいなかった」と批判。NATO事務総長は欧州諸国が合意を履行したと反論した。

articles.title: Iran war: Who gained ground, who lost influence
articles.description: While civilians in the region pay the highest price, the Iran war has reshaped power balances far beyond the Gulf. Some states have gained leverage. Others have lost influence.
articles.titleJa: イラン戦争：誰が優位に立ち、誰が影響力を失ったか
articles.summary: イラン戦争は、湾岸地域をはるかに超えて力の均衡を再編した。民間人が高い代償を払う中、一部の国は影響力を強め、他国は失った。

articles.title: Germany news: Lufthansa cabin crew to strike on Friday, UFO trade union says
articles.description: The latest strike to hit Germany's flagship carrier hails from the cabin crew union UFO. Meanwhile, Berlin is walking back a contentious plan about young men seeking permission to go abroad for long periods.
articles.titleJa: ドイツニュース：ルフトハンザ航空の客室乗務員、金曜日にストライキ実施へ
articles.summary: ドイツのルフトハンザ航空の客室乗務員組合UFOが金曜日のストライキを発表。また、ベルリンは若者の長期海外渡航許可に関する計画を撤回している。

articles.title: Norway's Telenor faces lawsuit for giving junta data from its customers in Myanmar
articles.description: A Swedish non-profit has filed a class action lawsuit against Norway's Telenor, accusing it of endangering customers in Myanmar by sharing their data with the junta. The company said it had no real choice.
articles.titleJa: ノルウェーのテレノール、ミャンマー顧客データ提供で軍事政権訴訟に直面
articles.summary: ノルウェーのテレノール社が、ミャンマーの顧客データを軍事政権に提供し顧客を危険に晒したとして、集団訴訟を起こされた。同社は選択の余地がなかったと主張。

articles.title: Nigeria: At least 20 killed in attack, residents say
articles.description: Authorities in Nigeria's restive north put the death toll at three, but residents and humanitarians point to a higher toll. The attack occurred in an area where kidnapping gangs and Islamist militants are active.
articles.titleJa: ナイジェリア：襲撃で少なくとも20人が死亡、住民が報告
articles.summary: ナイジェリア北部で襲撃があり、当局は死者3名と発表するも、住民らは少なくとも20名が死亡したと報告。誘拐集団やイスラム過激派が活動する地域での出来事。

articles.title: Iran doubles down on executions amid US-Israeli war
articles.description: Iran has executed 14 individuals in recent weeks, with the hangings continuing unabated regardless of the US-Israeli military campaign against the country.
articles.titleJa: イラン、米イスラエル戦争中に処刑を強化
articles.summary: イランは、米イスラエルによる軍事作戦中にもかかわらず、ここ数週間で14人の個人を処刑した。処刑は依然として継続中。

articles.title: JD Vance criticizes Zelenskyy and Ukraine in Hungary on visit campaigning for Viktor Orban
articles.description: US Vice President JD Vance has criticized EU leaders and Ukraine's Volodymyr Zelenskyy during his visit to Hungary, echoing Prime Minister Viktor Orban's campaign messaging. The Kremlin chimed in from Moscow, too.
articles.titleJa: JDヴァンス副大統領、ハンガリー訪問中にゼレンスキーとウクライナを批判
articles.summary: 米副大統領JDヴァンスがハンガリー訪問中、オルバン首相の選挙活動を支持し、ゼレンスキーとウクライナ、EU指導者を批判。クレムリンもコメントした。

articles.title: How Pakistan brokered a US-Iran ceasefire, and what's next
articles.description: The US, Israel and Iran have agreed to cease hostilities for two weeks after Islamabad mediated a temporary truce. The deal is a major achievement for Pakistan, but what comes next is likely to be even more complicated.
articles.titleJa: パキスタンはいかに米イラン停戦を仲介したか、そして次に来るもの
articles.summary: イスラマバードの仲介により、米国、イスラエル、イランが2週間の停戦に合意。これはパキスタンにとって大きな成果だが、今後の展開はさらに複雑になると予想される。

articles.title: Iran war: how farmers adapt as fertilizer crunch heats up
articles.description: The war in Iran has sparked a global fertilizer shortage and is threatening food security. What are farmers around the world doing to save their crops?
articles.titleJa: イラン戦争：肥料不足が深刻化する中、農家はいかに適応しているか
articles.summary: イラン戦争が世界的な肥料不足を引き起こし、食料安全保障を脅かしている。世界中の農家が作物を守るためにどう対応しているかを探る。

articles.title: US seeks to deport Kilmar Ábrego García to Liberia despite new Costa Rica deal
articles.description:
Man born in El Salvador has been fighting removal to series of ‘third’ countries after mistaken deportation last year
US government attorneys on Tuesday told a federal judge the Department of Homeland Security still intends to deport Kilmar Ábrego García to Liberia, despite a new agreement with Costa Rica to accept deportees who cannot legally be returned to their home countries.
The Salvadorian national’s case has become a focal point in the immigration debate after he was mistakenly deported to El Salvador last year. Since his return, he has been fighting a second deportation to a series of African countries proposed by homeland security officials.
 Continue reading...
articles.titleJa: 米国、コスタリカとの新たな合意にもかかわらず、キルマル・アブレゴ・ガルシア氏をリベリアへ送還しようとする
articles.summary: 米国政府は、誤ってエルサルバドルへ送還されたキルマル・アブレゴ・ガルシア氏を、コスタリカとの新たな送還合意があるにもかかわらず、リベリアへ送還する意向を示した。彼の事例は移民問題の焦点となっている。

articles.title: ‘We still deserve due process,’ says Cambodian man deported by US to Eswatini
articles.description:
Pheap Rom was one of 15 people sent to prison in African kingdom last year despite completing US sentences
A Cambodian man deported by the US said he would have accepted being sent to Cambodia, but instead ended up imprisoned in Eswatini, a country he knew so little about that when he first read the name he thought it was another immigration detention centre in Louisiana.
Pheap Rom, who had been convicted of attempted murder, was one of 10 deportees sent to Eswatini by the US in October 2025. They joined a group of five men, from Cambodia, Cuba, Jamaica, Vietnam and Yemen, who were deported to the small southern African country in July. All were sent to a maximum-security prison. Rom was deported from Eswatini to Cambodia in March.
 Continue reading...
articles.titleJa: 「我々には依然として適正な手続きを受ける権利がある」米国からエスワティニに強制送還されたカンボジア人男性が語る
articles.summary: 米国からエスワティニに強制送還され刑務所に収監されたカンボジア人男性は、適正な手続きを訴える。彼は故国カンボジアではなく、ほとんど知らないアフリカの国へ送られた。

articles.title: People of Burkina Faso should forget about democracy, says military ruler
articles.description:
Ibrahim Traoré, who took power in 2022 coup, tells state broadcaster ‘we must tell the truth, democracy isn’t for us’
People in Burkina Faso should forget about democracy as it is “not for us”, the military president, Ibrahim Traoré, told the country’s state broadcaster.
Traoré took power in a coup in September 2022, toppling another junta that had taken power just nine months earlier. He has since stifled opposition and in January banned political parties outright.
 Continue reading...
articles.titleJa: ブルキナファソ国民は民主主義を忘れるべき、と軍事指導者が発言
articles.summary: ブルキナファソの軍事大統領イブラヒム・トラオレは、国民に対し民主主義を忘れるべきだと語った。彼は2022年のクーデターで権力を掌握し、反対派を抑圧している。

articles.title: Uganda receives first US deportation flight under third-country agreement
articles.description:
Dozen people arrive under new deal but legal challenges expected with scheme criticised as ‘dehumanising process’
A flight carrying people being deported from the US has landed in Uganda, as Donald Trump’s administration pushes on with its strategy of expelling migrants to countries they have no ties to.
The deported people would stay in the east African country as “a transition phase for potential onward transmission to other countries”, an unnamed senior Ugandan government official told Reuters.
 Continue reading...
articles.titleJa: ウガンダ、第三国合意に基づき米国からの初の強制送還便を受け入れ
articles.summary: 米国からの強制送還便がウガンダに到着し、第三国との合意に基づく最初の措置となった。この計画は「非人道的プロセス」と批判され、法的異議申し立てが予想される。

articles.title: News outlets falsely report Somaliland called for extradition of Ilhan Omar
articles.description:
Reports, based on X post from unofficial account, follow JD Vance’s accusations and threats of finding ‘legal remedies’

Sign up for the Breaking News US email to get newsletter alerts in your inbox

Several news outlets have falsely reported that Somaliland’s government called for the extradition of Ilhan Omar, basing their stories on a post from an X account that does not represent the state despite its claims to the contrary.
Fox News, the New York Post, Sinclair Broadcast Group’s the National News Desk and the Independent ran stories on the US representative. The reports centred on a post by @RepOfSomaliland in reaction to claims by JD Vance that Omar had committed immigration fraud, which echoed prior allegations against the Somali-born Minnesota Democrat that she has vehemently denied.
 Continue reading...
articles.titleJa: 報道機関、ソマリランドがイルハン・オマル氏の身柄引き渡しを要求したと誤報
articles.summary: 複数の報道機関が、ソマリランド政府がイルハン・オマル米下院議員の身柄引き渡しを要求したと誤報した。非公式なXアカウントの投稿に基づくもので、米国の政治的対立と情報操作の問題を浮き彫りにする。

articles.title: Chile’s far-right government rips up plan for memorial at Pinochet torture site
articles.description:
New administration reverses expropriation of property founded by ex-Nazi Paul Schäfer, leaving victims in limbo
With its Germanic crosses and colourful toy-town facades, the village square of the tiny Chilean settlement of Villa Baviera gives little indication of the horrors of its past.
Until 1991, this cattle town of a few hundred people was a compound known as Colonia Dignidad. Its leader, Paul Schäfer, a former Nazi and weapons smuggler, bought a swathe of land in the valley in 1961, eventually holding as many as 300 people in a fenced enclave with minimal contact with the outside world. He sexually abused and even tortured the children in the camp.
 Continue reading...
articles.titleJa: チリの極右政権、ピノチェト時代の拷問施設の記念碑計画を撤回
articles.summary: チリの極右政権が、元ナチス党員パウル・シェーファーが設立したコロニア・ディグニダードの記念碑建設計画を撤回。この場所はピノチェト独裁政権下の拷問施設として知られ、犠牲者への影響が懸念される。

articles.title: Commonwealth leaders vow to keep seeking reparations after Reform UK plan to halt visas
articles.description:
Politicians warn party’s pledge to ‘punish’ countries seeking justice for slavery will harm and isolate Britain
Commonwealth politicians say they will not back down from seeking reparations as UK public figures, including a former Reform insider, warn the rightwing party’s pledge to “punish” countries seeking justice for slavery would harm and isolate Britain.
This week, Reform UK said they would halt visas for nationals of countries formally demanding reparations from Britain if they took power.
 Continue reading...
articles.titleJa: 英国改革党のビザ停止計画後も賠償請求を継続すると英連邦首脳が表明
articles.summary: 英国改革党が奴隷制度の賠償を求める国々へのビザを停止する計画を発表後、英連邦首脳は賠償請求を続けると表明。この政策は英国を孤立させると警告されている。

articles.title: Stork warning: woman gives birth midair on Jamaica-to-New York flight
articles.description:
Baby was delivered during Caribbean Airlines flight from Kingston to the US; nationality of child to be determined

Sign up for the Breaking News US email to get newsletter alerts in your inbox

A routine passenger flight from Jamaica landed at New York’s John F Kennedy international airport with one more person than it took off with after a woman gave birth in midair, potentially setting up a tricky situation over the newborn’s citizenship.
The “medical event” occurred on a Caribbean Airlines flight from Kingston on Saturday, according to a news release from the carrier.
 Continue reading...
articles.titleJa: コウノトリ警報：ジャマイカからニューヨーク行きのフライトで女性が機内で出産
articles.summary: ジャマイカ発ニューヨーク行きの機内で女性が出産し、フライトに一人増えて着陸した。新生児の国籍が未定で、複雑な問題が生じる可能性がある。

articles.title: First Nation asks court to block Alberta referendum on seceding from Canada
articles.description:
Sturgeon Lake Cree Nation has asked a court to halt the separatist push, arguing it would violate their treaty rights
A First Nation in Alberta has said that a separatist push for the province to secede from Canada is “consummately irresponsible and dishonourable” and should be shut down, arguing in court that a proposed referendum would violate their treaty rights.
A minority of residents of the oil-rich province have long argued that the province’s woes are due to the structure of payments to the federal government and a perceived inability to get their vast fossil fuel reserves to market.
 Continue reading...
articles.titleJa: 先住民、カナダからのアルバータ州分離独立に関する住民投票差し止めを裁判所に要求
articles.summary: アルバータ州の分離独立を巡る住民投票に対し、スタージョンレイク・クリー・ネイションが条約上の権利侵害を理由に裁判所へ差し止めを要求。カナダ国内の重要な政治問題となっている。

articles.title: Row over ‘virtual gated community’ AI surveillance plan in Toronto neighbourhood
articles.description:
Rosedale residents considering car licence plate-scanning Flock system in bid to tackle property crime
A row has broken out in one of Canada’s wealthiest neighbourhoods over plans to use an AI-powered surveillance system to create the country’s first “virtual gated community” to combat surging property crime.
Crime rates in Toronto as a whole are dropping but residents of Rosedale have been left on edge by a sustained rise in home invasions, with robbers targeting the tree-lined neighbourhood at a rate more than double the city average. Break-ins and thefts remain the third highest per capita in Toronto.
 Continue reading...
articles.titleJa: トロントの近隣地区で「仮想ゲーテッドコミュニティ」AI監視計画を巡り論争
articles.summary: トロントの裕福な地区で、財産犯罪対策としてAI搭載監視システム導入計画が浮上し、プライバシー侵害の懸念から論争になっている。これはカナダ初の「仮想ゲーテッドコミュニティ」となる可能性がある。

articles.title: Man rescued after two weeks trapped in collapsed Mexico mine
articles.description: A man trapped for nearly two weeks in a flooded gold mine in Sinaloa, Mexico, has been rescued by military divers.
articles.titleJa: メキシコの崩壊した鉱山で2週間閉じ込められた男性が救出
articles.summary: メキシコ・シナロア州の浸水した金鉱で2週間近く閉じ込められていた男性が、軍のダイバーによって救出された。

articles.title: Trump slams NATO over Iran after meeting Rutte, renews Greenland threat
articles.description: US president meets NATO chief, expresses disappointment over member states failing to back war on Iran.
articles.titleJa: トランプ氏、ルッテ首相と会談後、イラン巡りNATOを非難し、グリーンランドへの脅威を再表明
articles.summary: トランプ米大統領はNATO事務総長と会談し、加盟国がイランとの戦争を支持しないことに失望を表明、グリーンランドに関する脅威も再提起した。

articles.title: 13-year-old girl captures terrifying moment Israel bombed Beirut
articles.description: A 13-year-old girl on Snapchat captured the moment Israel began its assault on Beirut.
articles.titleJa: 13歳の少女がイスラエルによるベイルート爆撃の恐ろしい瞬間を捉える
articles.summary: 13歳の少女がスナップチャットで、イスラエルがベイルートへの攻撃を開始した瞬間を撮影した。その映像は衝撃を与えている。

articles.title: Turkish convoy condemns ‘lawless aggression’ of the US and Israel
articles.description: Hundreds of vehicles have driven through Istanbul to condemn the ‘lawless aggression’ of the US and Israel.
articles.titleJa: トルコの車列が米国とイスラエルの「不法な侵略」を非難
articles.summary: 数百台の車両がイスタンブールを走行し、米国とイスラエルによる「不法な侵略」を非難する抗議活動を行った。

articles.title: Protesters outside Israeli embassy in London condemn assault on Lebanon
articles.description: Protesters have blocked roads outside the Israeli embassy in London, condemning Israel’s violent strikes on Lebanon.
articles.titleJa: ロンドンのイスラエル大使館前で抗議活動、レバノン攻撃を非難
articles.summary: ロンドンのイスラエル大使館前で抗議者が道路を封鎖し、レバノンへのイスラエル軍の攻撃を非難した。国際的な非難が続く。

articles.title: US VP JD Vance says Lebanon is not part of the US-Iran ceasefire
articles.description: US Vice President JD Vance says Lebanon is not part of the US-Iran ceasefire.
articles.titleJa: 米副大統領JD・ヴァンス、レバノンは米・イラン停戦に含まれないと発言
articles.summary: 米国のJD・ヴァンス副大統領は、レバノンが米国とイランの停戦協定の対象外であると発言した。中東情勢の複雑さを浮き彫りにする。

articles.title: Iran war live: Trump threatens action if Tehran fails to comply with deal
articles.description: UN human rights chief Volker Turk describes Israel's attacks on Lebanon as 'horrific', coming so soon after ceasefire.
articles.titleJa: イラン戦争速報：トランプ氏、テヘランが合意を遵守しなければ行動を脅迫
articles.summary: トランプ氏はイランが合意に違反すれば行動を起こすと警告。国連人権高等弁務官は、停戦直後のイスラエルによるレバノン攻撃を「恐ろしい」と非難した。

articles.title: ‘Israel must be restrained for US-Iran ceasefire to hold’
articles.description: Former US counterterrorism chief Joe Kent had warned Israel could ‘sabotage’ the US-Iran ceasefire.
articles.titleJa: 「米・イラン停戦を維持するにはイスラエルを抑制する必要がある」
articles.summary: 元米国対テロ責任者のジョー・ケント氏は、イスラエルが米・イラン停戦を「妨害」する可能性があると警告した。停戦の行方に懸念が示されている。

articles.title: Woman given 15 years for role supplying drugs linked to Matthew Perry death
articles.description: Drug dealer who supplied dose that eventually led to Perry's death in 2023 pleaded guilty to five felony drug counts.
articles.titleJa: マシュー・ペリーの死に関連する薬物供給に関与した女性に15年の刑
articles.summary: 俳優マシュー・ペリーの死につながる薬物を供給したディーラーの女性が、5件の重罪薬物容疑を認め、15年の刑が言い渡された。

articles.title: Americans question Trump’s instinct on Iran war
articles.description: Americans are split on whether to trust Donald Trump's instinct-driven approach to the Iran conflict.
articles.titleJa: イラン戦争に関するトランプ氏の直感にアメリカ国民が疑問
articles.summary: イラン紛争に対するドナルド・トランプ氏の直感的なアプローチを信頼するかどうかについて、アメリカ国民の間で意見が分かれている。

articles.title: Bowen: Ceasefire means respite for civilians, but it might not last long
articles.description: Whether or not a lasting peace deal can be reached, the war and its consequences are reshaping the Middle East, writes international editor Jeremy Bowen.
articles.titleJa: ボーエン氏：停戦は市民に一時的な休息をもたらすも、長くは続かない可能性
articles.summary: 中東の戦争と和平交渉は地域を再形成しており、停戦は市民に一時的な休息を与えるものの、長くは続かない可能性があると報じられている。

articles.title: Negotiators face huge task to close gaps in rival Iran peace proposals
articles.description: A US 15-point plan and an Iranian 10-point variant are oceans apart, writes the BBC's diplomatic correspondent.
articles.titleJa: 交渉担当者、イランの対立する和平案の隔たりを埋めるため困難な課題に直面

articles.title: Israel backs US-Iran ceasefire but Netanyahu's war goals remain unfulfilled
articles.description: The Israeli prime minister's response to the ceasefire has been muted, in contrast to the triumphal statements from the US and Iran.
articles.titleJa: イスラエルは米イラン停戦を支持するも、ネタニヤフ首相の戦争目標は未達成
articles.summary: イスラエルは米イラン停戦を支持しているが、ネタニヤフ首相の戦争目標は達成されておらず、その反応は控えめだったと報じられている。

articles.title: Why ceasefire deal with US has unsettled Iran's hardliners
articles.description: The two-week truce opens up the prospect of direct talks with the US but has angered Iran's hardliners, writes BBC News Persian’s Kasra Naji.
articles.titleJa: 米国との停戦合意がイランの強硬派を動揺させている理由
articles.summary: 米国との2週間の停戦合意は、直接対話の可能性を開いたが、イランの強硬派を激怒させている理由が報じられている。

articles.title: How Pakistan helped secure a fragile ceasefire between the US and Iran
articles.description: Pakistan has a historic relationship with Iran, but an agreement was far from certain, writes the BBC’s Caroline Davies
articles.titleJa: パキスタンが米国とイラン間の不安定な停戦確保にどのように貢献したか
articles.summary: パキスタンが米国とイランの間の不安定な停戦を確保するために歴史的な関係を活かして仲介に貢献した経緯が報じられている。

articles.title: Oil prices plunge and shares jump on US-Iran ceasefire plan
articles.description: Crude prices tumbled by as much as 15% on the conditional pause but remain far higher than before the war.
articles.titleJa: 米イラン停戦計画を受け原油価格が急落し株価が急騰

articles.title: US journalist Shelly Kittleson released after kidnap in Iraq, officials say

articles.titleJa: 米国人ジャーナリスト、シェリー・キットルソン氏がイラクでの誘拐後解放されたと当局が発表

articles.title: French couple leave Iran after more than three years in jail

articles.title: One gunman killed and two injured in shooting at Israeli consulate in Istanbul

articles.titleJa: イスタンブールのイスラエル領事館で銃撃、犯人1名死亡、2名負傷

articles.title: WHO suspends Gaza medical evacuations after contractor killed by Israeli troops
articles.description: Israel's military says troops fired at a vehicle driven by the Palestinian because they believed it posed "an immediate threat".
articles.titleJa: WHO、イスラエル軍による請負業者殺害を受けガザの医療避難を一時停止

articles.title: Ringleader of suspected human trafficking network arrested in Ethiopia
articles.description: Yetbarek Dawit is alleged to have operated a multi-million-dollar operation, using torture to raise money.

articles.title: Dozens killed as Angola flood death toll rises

articles.title: Carnival fever hits Lagos as locals celebrate Afro-Brazilian heritage

articles.title: 'I adore her now': Mother learns to live with child's autism in a country with little help

articles.title: 'We want a voice in our land' - the people evicted to build Nigeria's capital

articles.titleJa: 「我々の土地で声を上げたい」－ナイジェリアの首都建設で立ち退かされた人々
articles.summary: ナイジェリアの首都建設のために立ち退かされた人々が、政府が約束を履行しないと非難し、自分たちの土地での発言権を求めています。

articles.title: One ant for $220: The new frontier of wildlife trafficking
articles.description: The craze for collecting ants takes Kenya by surprise as smugglers zone in to make a profit.
articles.titleJa: アリ一匹220ドル：野生生物密売の新たなフロンティア

articles.title: 'The gravest crime against humanity': What does the UN vote on slavery mean?
articles.description: African and Caribbean nations want countries which benefited from slavery to pay compensation but it will not be straightforward.
articles.titleJa: 「人類に対する最も重大な犯罪」：国連の奴隷制度に関する投票は何を意味するのか？

articles.title: Women secretly filmed, then ridiculed and abused online

articles.title: Rationing power and diluting petrol - how African countries are coping with effects of Iran war

articles.title: Inside the alleged Russian operation to trigger anti-government protests in Angola
articles.description: A group of Russians and Angolans are going on trial in Angola after being accused of fomenting protests, which they deny.
articles.titleJa: アンゴラでの反政府デモを扇動したとされるロシアの作戦の内幕

articles.title: DRC: Urban trees are natural CO₂ stores that merit closer attention
articles.description: Urban trees are not just landscaping features. They are natural carbon sinks, capable of offsetting human activity-related carbon dioxide (CO₂) emissions.

articles.title: How initiatives to narrow Africa’s digital gap are affecting women’s representation
articles.description: Africa Wiki Women encourages African women’s advancement in online knowledge creation and sharing through training, contribution campaigns, and mentorship programs.

articles.title: How AI is used to influence global elections
articles.description: Companies that work in political persuasion are experimenting with AI at scale to see how they can make the traditional influencing methods even cheaper, faster, more convincing, and more automated.

articles.title: Unpacking the political complexities of the Benue killings in Nigeria
articles.description: What we are dealing with here in Benue is a calculated, well planned, full-scale genocidal invasion and land grabbing campaign by Herder terrorists and bandits, which ... is worsening every year.

articles.title: Cameroonian rice farmers caught between national ambition and rural realities
articles.description: The Cameroonian government’s rice self-sufficiency policy has sparked enthusiasm among the country’s rice farmers. In the northwest, rice farmers combine courage with determination every day to fulfil these national promises.

articles.title: African women make economic gains through global gig economy
articles.description: For decades, economic opportunity was often tied to migration. Today, millions of Africans are plugging directly into global commerce from their homes and building careers that span continents.

articles.title: Climate change brings changes to North Kivu’s agricultural practices in the DRC
articles.description: Climate change has impacted North Kivu, a region in the eastern Democratic Republic of the Congo (DRC), where farmers have had to adapt their farming practices to survive.

articles.title: François Kaserake Kamate on global complicity and the fight for the DRC
articles.description: "I was of violence, war and the cycle of social injustices. I wanted to follow a non-violent path because I believed in peaceful strategies to change the paradigm."

articles.title: From menstrual dignity to digital safety: How grassroots feminists are redefining gender justice
articles.description: What connects a pad box in a Nigerian school, a coalition meeting in Pakistan, and a digital security workshop in Paraguay? They each address a structural barrier that limits women’s participation.

articles.title: Ethiopia’s urban renewal projects and turn toward aesthetic propaganda
articles.description: This is not a trend limited to Ethiopia. Influencers have increasingly become important in popularizing selective authoritarian stories from Dubai to China, and thereby legitimizing authoritarian narratives.

articles.title: JD Vance backs Orbán's re-election bid in Budapest visit and hits out at EU
articles.description: US Vice-President JD Vance intervenes in the campaign to give Orbán a ringing endorsement in Budapest.

articles.description: The lorry driver is in custody after the train driver died during the collision in northern France.

articles.summary: フランス北部で列車とトラックが衝突し、列車運転士が死亡。トラック運転士は拘留された。

articles.title: The US refinery now processing Venezuelan oil

articles.title: In one of country's poorest states, Venezuelans hope for post-Maduro boom

articles.title: Cuba's mothers-to-be prepare to give birth in a country plunged into darkness

articles.title: Community micro-hydropower plants revolutionise isolated areas of the Dominican Republic
articles.description: Community micro-hydropower projects in the DR rely on local leadership to deliver energy from renewable sources to isolated areas excluded from national distribution systems, contributing to reduced greenhouse gas emissions.

articles.title: How Bad Bunny brought the issue of Puerto Rico’s power grid into world view

articles.title: Ecuador’s Netanyahu and Trump supporters target Ecuadorian–Iranian Cultural Center
articles.description: Wars no longer have defined frontlines. Battlefields can be anywhere those in power decide that a neighbor, due to their faith, ethnicity, or background, is an enemy.

articles.title: Concerns grow as Jamaica ends Cuban medical programme

articles.title: How far-right ‘fear tactics’ affect girls seeking legal abortion in Brazil
articles.description: Fighting a resolution to ensure access to legal abortion for girls under 14 is the latest example of abortion rights being used as a political tool by conservatives in the country

articles.title: Nawat language now has its own Wikipedia

articles.title: Beyond words: Incarcerated women’s responses to punitive systems in Peru
articles.description: A collage-based artistic creation workshop revealed how a group of incarcerated women counter the dynamics and effects of imprisonment through individual reflection, creative practice, and the sustaining of collective bonds.

articles.title: Action images from Venezuela serve as political messaging and narrative control for Trump
articles.description: For professor Clotilde Perez, spoken to by Pública, contemporary geopolitics has become “media-performative”, where no image is neutral.

articles.title: Mexican government partially unblocks secure internet
articles.description: The Tor network is used by millions of users daily to securely access the internet, safeguard their right to information and freedom of expression, and avoid censorship or government surveillance.

articles.title: Healing a region: How healthcare is bringing peace to Arauca, Colombia
articles.description: Reliable, inclusive healthcare does more than treat illness. It can function as social glue that softens community strain and transforms clinics into safe spaces for everyone.

articles.title: Key Indian state polls begin in test for Modi's party
articles.description: Modi's Bharatiya Janata Party is trying to expand into regions where it has struggled to gain a foothold.
articles.titleJa: インドの主要州議会選挙、モディ党にとって試練の開始
articles.summary: インドでモディ首相率いる人民党にとって重要となる州議会選挙が始まり、同党はこれまで足がかりを得られなかった地域への進出を目指す。

articles.title: Is BTS losing its K-pop identity as it aims for a bigger world stage?
articles.description: BTS turned millions on to K-pop. But now it’s caught between Korea and the world.
articles.titleJa: BTSはより大きな世界舞台を目指す中でK-POPとしてのアイデンティティを失っているのか？
articles.summary: K-POPを世界に広めたBTSが、韓国と世界の間で板挟みになり、K-POPとしてのアイデンティティを失う可能性について議論されている。

articles.title: Chinese researcher's death after questioning in US prompts anger in Beijing
articles.description: The semiconductor researcher was found dead after “hostile questioning” by US law enforcement.
articles.titleJa: 米国での尋問後の中国人研究者の死、北京で怒りを引き起こす
articles.summary: 米国で半導体研究者が米法執行機関による「敵対的な尋問」後に死亡し、中国政府はこれに対し強い怒りを示している。

articles.title: British teen dies in reported Vietnam motorbike fall
articles.description: Orla Wates is remembered by her mother as "beautiful, independent and very funny".
articles.titleJa: ベトナムでのバイク転倒事故で英国人ティーンが死亡と報道
articles.summary: ベトナムで英国人ティーンのオーラ・ウェイツさんがバイク転倒事故で死亡したと報じられ、母親は彼女を「美しく、自立した、とても面白い」と偲んでいる。

articles.title: Vietnam's leader To Lam strengthens power in unanimous assembly vote
articles.description: Vietnam's communist party chief is now also the country's president - an unusual concentration of power.
articles.titleJa: ベトナムのトー・ラム指導者、議会で満場一致の投票により権力を強化
articles.summary: ベトナムの共産党書記長であるトー・ラム氏が、異例の権力集中により同国の大統領も兼任し、権力を強化した。

articles.title: Air India CEO steps down early as losses mount
articles.description: Wilson, whose term was set to end in 2027, will remain CEO and MD until a successor is appointed.
articles.titleJa: 損失拡大を受け、エア・インディアCEOが早期退任
articles.summary: エア・インディアのCEOウィルソン氏が、損失の増加を受けて任期を前倒しで辞任するが、後任が指名されるまで職務を継続する。

articles.title: India's top court hears challenges to ruling on women's entry into temple
articles.description: Experts say the court's decision will have far-reaching consequences for women's religious freedoms in India.
articles.titleJa: インド最高裁、女性の寺院入場に関する判決への異議を審理
articles.summary: インド最高裁は、女性の宗教的自由にとって大きな影響を与えるとされる、女性の寺院入場に関する以前の判決に対する異議申し立てを審理している。

articles.title: Nine policemen sentenced to death in India over Covid custody killings
articles.description: The father and son died in jail after being arrested on suspicion of breaching Covid lockdown rules in 2020.
articles.titleJa: インド、新型コロナ巡る拘留中の殺害で警官9人に死刑判決
articles.summary: インドで、新型コロナウイルス感染症のロックダウン規則違反容疑で逮捕された父子が拘留中に死亡した事件で、警官9人に死刑判決が下された。

articles.title: Emergency jabs after 100 children die of suspected measles in a month in Bangladesh
articles.description: More than 100 people, mostly children, have been killed by measles since mid-March, officials suspect.
articles.titleJa: バングラデシュで麻疹疑いで子供100人以上死亡後、緊急予防接種
articles.summary: バングラデシュで麻疹と疑われる病気により1ヶ月で100人以上の子供が死亡したことを受け、緊急予防接種が開始された。

articles.title: Trump's deadline looms but Asian nations already have deals with Iran
articles.description: Nations in the region have been keen to reach agreements as their economies are heavily reliant on Middle East energy.
articles.titleJa: トランプ氏の期限が迫るも、アジア諸国は既にイランと取引
articles.summary: トランプ氏が設定した期限が迫る中、アジア諸国は中東のエネルギーに大きく依存しているため、既にイランとの間で取引を進めている。

articles.title: Members participating in IFD Agreement issue joint ministerial declaration at end of MC14
articles.description: The 129 WTO members participating in the Investment Facilitation for Development Agreement (IFDA) issued a joint ministerial declaration on 29 March at the end of the 14th Ministerial Conference in Yaoundé, Cameroon. A joint press release by the IFDA co-Coordinators — the Republic of Korea and Chile — is provided below.

articles.title: MC14 concludes with adopted decisions, progress on key outstanding issues
articles.description: The WTO's 14th Ministerial Conference (MC14) concluded early 30 March with ministers adopting a number of decisions as well as making a commitment to continue work in Geneva on key outstanding issues.

articles.title: DG cites MPIA as "practical, confidence-building bridge" pending dispute reform deal
articles.description: The Multi-Party Interim Appeal Arbitration Arrangement (MPIA) is serving as a practical, confidence building bridge for WTO members committed to a fully functioning dispute settlement system pending an agreement on dispute settlement reform, Director-General Ngozi Okonjo-Iweala said on 28 March. She made her remarks at a meeting of parties to the MPIA on the sidelines of the WTO's 14th Ministerial Conference (MC14).

articles.title: Ministers exchange views on key WTO topics, consider paths forward at MC14
articles.description: Ministers and senior officials attending the WTO's 14th Ministerial Conference (MC14) participated in a series of meetings on 28 March to address key issues on the WTO agenda and to provide political direction for the post-MC14 work ahead.

articles.title: Members adopt a pathway to bring E‑Commerce Agreement into force via interim arrangements
articles.description: Sixty-six members, covering approximately 70% of global trade, have adopted a pathway to bring into force the WTO Agreement on Electronic Commerce through interim arrangements while continuing to work towards its incorporation into the WTO legal framework of rules, co-convenors announced on 28 March at the 14th Ministerial Conference taking place in Yaoundé, Cameroon. The joint press release is below.

articles.title: Adding value to cotton in Africa can drive inclusive growth, ministers and agency heads say
articles.description: Investment in the cotton, textiles and garments sector can help transform economic development in Central and West Africa, said speakers at a high-level event on the eve of the WTO's 14th Ministerial Conference in Yaoundé, Cameroon, on 25 March and at the opening ceremony on 26 March. Ministers, heads of international agencies, development finance institutions, private sector partners and FIFA representatives highlighted the opportunities for Africa to weave its way up the value chain to accelerate economic growth.

articles.title: Australia contributes an additional AUD 2 million to WTO Fish Fund
articles.description: The government of Australia has committed an additional AUD 2 million (approximately CHF 1.2 million) to the WTO Fisheries Funding Mechanism, known as the Fish Fund, to assist developing and least-developed members in implementing the Agreement on Fisheries Subsidies. This contribution builds on Australia's earlier contribution of AUD 2 million in May 2023.

articles.title: EU launches safeguard investigation on grain-oriented electrical steel
articles.description: On 27 March 2026, the European Union notified the WTO’s Committee on Safeguards that it had initiated, on the same day (27 March 2026), a safeguard investigation on imports of grain-oriented electrical steel.

articles.title: Africa, Latin America strengthen economic and trade relations at MC14 side event
articles.description: A high-level meeting dedicated to reinforcing cooperation and trade relations between Africa and Latin America was held on the margins of the WTO's 14th Ministerial Conference (MC14) on 27 March. In a keynote address, Director-General Ngozi Okonjo-Iweala highlighted the importance of this initiative to diversify trade and continue improving South-South trade.

articles.title: Plastics Dialogue coordinators issue MC14 ministerial statement, technical outcome package
articles.description: On 26 March, the opening day of the 14th Ministerial Conference (MC14), the six co-coordinators of the Dialogue on Plastics Pollution and Environmentally Sustainable Plastics Trade (DPP) — Australia, Barbados, China, Ecuador, Fiji and Morocco — presented a ministerial statement and a package of five technical documents. Together, they reflect the progress achieved by participating members since MC13 and set out a forward-looking roadmap for trade-related cooperation to address plastics pollution.
